name: First Med
districts_or_customers: Likely NC Medicaid/NEMT; Sampson County area
recent_news: No M&A or growth news found (2024-2026).
website: firstmedinc.com (note: domain currently appears to host unrelated online pharmacy content; original transport business may be offline or rebranded)
fit_notes: Very small (~19 employees, ~$1.3M est revenue) and appears to be NEMT/medical transport rather than school bus contracting. Website concern (domain repurposed) raises viability question. Poor fit for Summit school-bus tuck-in absent confirmation of school district contracts.

name: AAA Medex
owner_or_parent: Yellow Cab of Arizona / AAA Transportation (Tucson taxi cooperative)
districts_or_customers: Arizona AHCCCS Medicaid NEMT; Tucson-area elderly and disabled riders
recent_news: Part of Yellow Cab of Arizona conglomerate (which also owns AAA Cab, Courier Cab, Checker Cab, Neal's Cab, TLC Cab, Fiesta Taxi) since the 2002 AAA acquisition; positioned as largest NEMT operator in AZ.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus operator; part of a multi-brand taxi/NEMT holding company in Tucson. Poor fit for Summit student-transportation tuck-in (wrong service line, strategic-owned).

name: Key Transit
districts_or_customers: Denver metro nursing homes and individual wheelchair clients; no identified school-district contracts
recent_news: No M&A or notable news found (2024-2026); positions as 'most trusted wheelchair transportation company' in Denver metro.
fit_notes: Pure wheelchair NEMT operator (mileage-based fee model, Mon-Sun ops out of Englewood/Centennial). No school or SPED student-transport footprint identified. Poor fit for Summit school-bus tuck-in.

name: Around The Sound Trnsprtn
owner_or_parent: independent (ATS Trans, LLC / TransPro Inc., Hutchins family)
districts_or_customers: Pierce, King, Kitsap, Thurston, Lewis, Clallam, Jefferson, and Mason counties; service into ID/MT/OR; nursing homes, medical facilities, individual riders
recent_news: Family-owned and operated since 1998; positions as Tacoma's premier non-emergency transportation provider; 80+ vehicle fleet.
fit_notes: Independent, family-owned (Hutchins) PNW operator with credible 80+ vehicle fleet across WA/OR/ID/MT, but service mix is NEMT/airport/event rather than yellow school-bus or district SPED contracts. Adjacent-segment tuck-in only if Summit wants to expand into NEMT/SPED-style passenger transport in the Puget Sound corridor.

name: Care Trans
districts_or_customers: San Jose/Santa Clara County NEMT clients (likely Medi-Cal/Santa Clara Family Health Plan); no school-district contracts identified
recent_news: No M&A or notable news found. Website carecab.com did not resolve at research time; the 'Care Cab' brand appears to be used by a distinct, unrelated Minnesota operator.
fit_notes: Very small (27 employees, ~$2M est revenue), appears to be NEMT-focused in San Jose. No evidence of school-bus or SPED contracts. Poor fit for Summit student-transportation tuck-in absent confirmation of K-12 work.
